pi_items.source: task.json description + setup_task.sh channel configuration + export_result.sh get_channel_id call + verifier.py channel validation
export_result.sh: nc -w 2 localhost 6661 (line 22) + setup_task.sh terminal output description 'TCP Listener on port 6661'
setup_task.sh: HL7 PID segment construction: PID|1||PAT78432^^^HOSP^MR||SMITH^JANE^A||... (line 72, MRN in PID-3.1)
setup_task.sh: file creation with open('/home/ga/test_data/test_oru_message.hl7', 'w') (line 77) + export_result.sh: file reference at line 14
setup_task.sh: PDF content construction starting with b'%PDF-1.1\n' (line 28) + export_result.sh: magic byte validation with head -c 4 | grep -q '%PDF' (line 77)

privileged_info_summary: Task obx_attachment_extraction@1 extracts Base64-encoded PDF attachments from HL7 ORU messages. The channel 'Lab_Report_Extractor' listens on TCP port 6661 for MLLP-framed HL7 v2.x messages. PDF data is extracted from OBX-5.5 (5th component of OBX segment field 5), decoded from Base64, and written to /home/ga/lab_reports/ with filenames containing the patient MRN from PID-3.1 field. Test data uses synthetic PDF (starting with magic bytes %PDF) and HL7 message with MRN PAT78432 generated dynamically during setup.
